data.content:
### Configure API Key for Authentication

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.13.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Set the E2B API key for authentication purposes. If not provided, it defaults to the E2B_API_KEY environment variable.

```typescript
optional apiKey: string;

// E2B API key to use for authentication.
// Default: E2B_API_KEY // environment variable
```

--------------------------------

### E2B API Key Configuration

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.11.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Configures the E2B API key for authentication. It can be set directly or defaulted to the E2B_API_KEY environment variable.

```typescript
optional apiKey: string;
```

--------------------------------

### API Key Configuration

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.13.2/sandbox/page.mdx

Sets the E2B API key for authentication. This is a string option. Defaults to the E2B_API_KEY environment variable.

```typescript
optional apiKey: string;

// Default: E2B_API_KEY // environment variable
```

--------------------------------

### Configure API Keys for E2B and Claude

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/code-interpreting/analyze-data-with-ai/page.mdx

Sets up environment variables with E2B and Claude API keys. These keys are essential for authentication and accessing the respective services. Ensure these are stored securely in a .env file.

```bash
E2B_API_KEY=e2b_***
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-***
```

--------------------------------

### Create Sandbox

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.5.2/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance. You can specify a template, timeout, metadata, environment variables, and API key for authentication. It can also configure proxy settings and security.

```APIDOC
## POST /sandboxes

### Description
Creates a new sandbox.

### Method
POST

### Endpoint
/sandboxes

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **template** (string) - Optional - Sandbox template name or ID.
- **timeout** (integer) - Optional - Timeout for the sandbox in seconds (default: 300). Max 86400 for Pro, 3600 for Hobby.
- **metadata** (object) - Optional - Custom metadata for the sandbox.
- **envs** (object) - Optional - Custom environment variables for the sandbox.
- **api_key** (string) - Optional - E2B API Key (defaults to E2B_API_KEY env var).
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.
- **proxy** (ProxyTypes) - Optional - Proxy to use for the request and for requests made to the sandbox.
- **secure** (boolean) - Optional - Whether to secure the env with an access token.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "template": "default",
  "timeout": 600,
  "metadata": {
    "project_id": "123"
  },
  "envs": {
    "NODE_ENV": "production"
  }
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **sandbox_id** (string) - The ID of the newly created sandbox.
- **sandbox_url** (string) - The URL to access the sandbox.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "sandbox_id": "sbx_abcdef123456",
  "sandbox_url": "https://your-e2b-instance.com/sandboxes/sbx_abcdef123456"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Create New Sandbox Instance (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.9.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance, optionally from a specified template and with custom configurations like environment variables, metadata, and timeouts. It supports API key authentication and proxy settings. This is the primary method for initializing a sandbox.

```python
@classmethod
async def create(cls, template: Optional[str] = None, timeout: Optional[int] = None, metadata: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None, envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None, api_key: Optional[str] = None, domain: Optional[str] = None, debug: Optional[bool] = None, request_timeout: Optional[float] = None, proxy: Optional[ProxyTypes] = None, secure: Optional[bool] = None) -> 'AsyncSandbox':
    """
    Create a new sandbox.

    By default, the sandbox is created from the default `base` sandbox template.

    Arguments:
        template: Sandbox template name or ID
        timeout: Timeout for the sandbox in **seconds**, default to 300 seconds. Maximum time a sandbox can be kept alive is 24 hours (86_400 seconds) for Pro users and 1 hour (3_600 seconds) for Hobby users.
        metadata: Custom metadata for the sandbox
        envs: Custom environment variables for the sandbox
        api_key: E2B API Key to use for authentication, defaults to `E2B_API_KEY` environment variable
        request_timeout: Timeout for the request in **seconds**
        proxy: Proxy to use for the request and for the **requests made to the returned sandbox**
        secure: Envd is secured with access token and cannot be used without it

    Returns:
        sandbox instance for the new sandbox
    """
    pass
```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox API - List Sandboxes

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.1.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Lists all currently running sandboxes. Authentication can be provided via an API key or defaults to an environment variable.

```APIDOC
## GET /sandboxes

### Description
Lists all running sandboxes.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/sandboxes

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **api_key** (string) - Optional - API key to use for authentication, defaults to `E2B_API_KEY` environment variable.
- **domain** (string) - Optional - The domain to list sandboxes from.
- **debug** (boolean) - Optional - Enable debug mode for listing sandboxes.
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "api_key": "YOUR_API_KEY",
  "domain": "example.com",
  "debug": true,
  "request_timeout": 30.0
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **sandboxes** (array[SandboxInfo]) - List of running sandboxes.

##### SandboxInfo
- **name** (string) - The name of the sandbox.
- **id** (string) - The unique identifier of the sandbox.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "sandboxes": [
    {
      "name": "my-sandbox-1",
      "id": "sbx_123abc"
    },
    {
      "name": "another-sandbox",
      "id": "sbx_456def"
    }
  ]
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Create Sandbox with API Key (JavaScript, Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/api-key/page.mdx

Demonstrates how to create a new sandbox instance using an API key. The API key can be provided either as an environment variable (E2B_API_KEY) or directly to the Sandbox constructor. This snippet shows examples in both JavaScript and Python.

```js
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const sandbox = await Sandbox.create({ apiKey: 'YOUR_API_KEY' })
```

```python
from e2b_code_interpreter import Sandbox

sbx = Sandbox.create(api_key="YOUR_API_KEY")
```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox Initialization

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-python-sdk/v1.6.2/sandbox/page.mdx

Initializes a new desktop sandbox with customizable parameters such as resolution, DPI, template, and timeout. It also supports authentication via API key and domain, and can be run in debug mode.

```APIDOC
## Sandbox Initialization

### Description
Create a new desktop sandbox with customizable parameters.

### Method
```python
__init__(
    resolution: Optional[Tuple[int, int]] = None,
    dpi: Optional[int] = None,
    display: Optional[str] = None,
    template: Optional[str] = None,
    timeout: Optional[int] = None,
    metadata: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
    envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
    api_key: Optional[str] = None,
    domain: Optional[str] = None,
    debug: Optional[bool] = None,
    sandbox_id: Optional[str] = None,
    request_timeout: Optional[float] = None
)
```

### Parameters
#### Path Parameters
None

#### Query Parameters
None

#### Request Body
None

### Request Example
```python
from e2b import Sandbox

sandbox = Sandbox(template='nodejs', timeout=600)
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **sandbox_instance** (Sandbox) - An instance of the Sandbox class for the newly created sandbox.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "message": "Sandbox created successfully"
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox API - List Sandboxes

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.0.6/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Lists all running sandboxes associated with your account. Authentication can be provided via an API key or by using the E2B_API_KEY environment variable.

```APIDOC
## GET /sandboxes

### Description
List all running sandboxes.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/sandboxes

### Query Parameters
- **api_key** (string) - Optional - API key to use for authentication. Defaults to the E2B_API_KEY environment variable.
- **domain** (string) - Optional - Filter sandboxes by domain.
- **debug** (boolean) - Optional - Whether to enable debug mode for the sandboxes.
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **sandboxes** (array[SandboxInfo]) - A list of running sandbox information.
  - **sandbox_id** (string) - The unique identifier for the sandbox.
  - **domain** (string) - The domain associated with the sandbox.
  - **created_at** (string) - The timestamp when the sandbox was created.

### Response Example
```json
{
  "sandboxes": [
    {
      "sandbox_id": "sb-xxxxxxxxxxxx",
      "domain": "example.com",
      "created_at": "2023-10-27T10:00:00Z"
    }
  ]
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Connect to Existing Sandbox (Python)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.2.0/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Establishes a connection to a pre-existing sandbox using its unique ID. This allows interaction with the same sandbox from different environments or applications. An API key can be optionally provided for authentication, defaulting to the E2B_API_KEY environment variable.

```python
@classmethod
def connect(cls, sandbox_id: str, api_key: Optional[str] = None, domain: Optional[str] = None, debug: Optional[bool] = None):
    # ... implementation details ...
    pass

# Example Usage:
# Assuming 'sandbox' is an existing sandbox instance
sandbox_id = sandbox.sandbox_id
same_sandbox = Sandbox.connect(sandbox_id)
```

--------------------------------

### Get Sandbox Information using SandboxApi

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.5.4/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Fetches detailed information for a specific sandbox identified by its ID. Allows configuration of API key, domain, debug mode, request timeout, headers, and proxy. Defaults to environment variables for API key, domain, and debug settings.

```python
@classmethod
async def get_info(cls,
                   sandbox_id: str,
                   api_key: Optional[str] = None,
                   domain: Optional[str] = None,
                   debug: Optional[bool] = None,
                   request_timeout: Optional[float] = None,
                   headers: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
                   proxy: Optional[ProxyTypes] = None) -> SandboxInfo:
    # ... implementation details ...
```

--------------------------------

### Environment Variable Setup (.env)

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/quickstart/page.mdx

Example of how to store your E2B API key in a .env file for local development.

```bash
E2B_API_KEY=e2b_***
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript - Sandbox Configuration Options

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.12.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Defines optional configuration parameters for E2B sandboxes. These include settings for internet access, API keys, debugging, domain, custom environment variables and metadata, request and sandbox timeouts, and security.

```typescript
optional allowInternetAccess: boolean;
optional apiKey: string;
optional debug: boolean;
optional domain: string;
optional envs: Record<string, string>;
optional headers: Record<string, string>;
optional logger: Logger;
optional metadata: Record<string, string>;
optional requestTimeoutMs: number;
optional secure: boolean;
optional timeoutMs: number;
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize E2B Sandbox

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/desktop-python-sdk/v1.7.3/sandbox/page.mdx

Initializes a new desktop sandbox instance. Allows configuration of resolution, DPI, display, template, timeouts, environment variables, and authentication details. Defaults are provided for many parameters, and environment variables can be used for API keys and domains.

```python
class Sandbox(SandboxBase)
def __init__(resolution: Optional[Tuple[int, int]] = None,
             dpi: Optional[int] = None,
             display: Optional[str] = None,
             template: Optional[str] = None,
             timeout: Optional[int] = None,
             metadata: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
             envs: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
             api_key: Optional[str] = None,
             domain: Optional[str] = None,
             debug: Optional[bool] = None,
             sandbox_id: Optional[str] = None,
             request_timeout: Optional[float] = None,
             proxy: Optional[ProxyTypes] = None)
```

--------------------------------

### Connect to an Existing Sandbox

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.0.0/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Establishes a connection to a pre-existing sandbox using its unique sandbox ID. This allows for interaction with a sandbox from different locations or environments. Authentication is managed via an API key or the E2B_API_KEY environment variable.

```python
@classmethod
def connect(cls,
            sandbox_id: str,
            api_key: Optional[str] = None,
            domain: Optional[str] = None,
            debug: Optional[bool] = None)
```
